canonical_name: Mahatma Jyotiba Phule Jan Arogya Yojana

local_names.hi: महात्मा ज्योतिबा फुले जन आरोग्य योजना
local_names.mr: महात्मा ज्योतिबा फुले जन आरोग्य योजना
local_names.en: Mahatma Jyotiba Phule People's Health Scheme

income_thresholds.description: Yellow ration card holders (Antyodaya Anna Yojana / AAY): income below Rs 1 lakh. Fully eligible.
Orange ration card holders: income between Rs 1-1.5 lakh. Fully eligible.

exclusion_rules.description: Only residents of Maharashtra are eligible
Must hold a Yellow (AAY), Orange, or White ration card issued by Maharashtra government. Saffron (APL above limit) card holders are not covered.

exclusion_rules.condition: occupation == 'farmer' and district not in drought_districts_14
exclusion_rules.description: Farmers are eligible in 14 designated drought-prone districts. Farmers in other districts must qualify through ration card path.

family_criteria.family_definition: Family as listed on the ration card. All members on the ration card are covered. Coverage is floater. For farmer families in drought districts, all household members are covered.

covered_procedures: Orthopedic (joint replacement, fracture surgery, spine surgery)
excluded_procedures: Organ transplantation (select types under separate scheme)

required_documents.name: Ration Card (Yellow / AAY / Orange / White)
required_documents.notes: Primary eligibility document. The specific color/type determines the coverage tier. Yellow and Orange cards are most common for this scheme.

required_documents.name: Income Certificate
alternatives: Ration Card serves as income proxy
required_documents.notes: May be required for white ration card holders or contested cases. Ration card color generally sufficient.

required_documents.name: Farmer Certificate (for drought district farmers)
required_documents.notes: Required only for farmers in 14 drought-prone districts. 7/12 land record extract from Talathi office.

required_documents.name: Mobile Number
required_documents.notes: For OTP verification and claim status updates

enrollment_steps: Step 1: Go to any government or empanelled hospital in Maharashtra with ration card and Aadhaar
Step 2: Visit the MJPJAY registration desk (Arogya Mitra counter) at the hospital
Step 3: Submit ration card for color/type verification in the system
Step 4: Complete Aadhaar e-KYC (biometric or OTP)
Step 5: If you are a farmer in a drought district, submit 7/12 extract or farmer certificate
Step 6: Registration is verified against Maharashtra ration card database
Step 7: Health card issued digitally. Can also apply online via Aaple Sarkar portal.
Step 8: Present card at any empanelled hospital for cashless treatment up to Rs 5 lakh (Rs 1.5 lakh for select procedures)
description_for_embedding: Mahatma Jyotiba Phule Jan Arogya Yojana (MJPJAY) is Maharashtra's state health insurance scheme providing Rs 5 lakh per family per year (Rs 1.5 lakh for some procedures). Eligibility is primarily through ration card color: Yellow (AAY), Orange, and White card holders qualify. Additionally, farmers in 14 designated drought-prone districts are covered regardless of ration card type. The scheme covers 1,100+ surgical and medical procedures at empanelled government and private hospitals across Maharashtra. Enrollment via government hospitals, Aaple Sarkar portal, or empanelled hospital Arogya Mitra desks.
